description: Transforms 'flex' shorthand to 'flex-grow', 'flex-shrink', 'flex-basis' longhands successfully.

edits.replace: .flex-container { display: flex; flex-grow: 1; flex-shrink: 0; flex-basis: auto; gap: 10px; }

rationale: The 'flex' shorthand is correctly expanded into its longhand properties, and the original shorthand is removed.

description: Fails when 'flex' shorthand is partially expanded, missing 'flex-basis'.

edits.replace: .flex-container { display: flex; flex-grow: 1; flex-shrink: 0; gap: 10px; }

rationale: The 'flex-basis' property is implicitly '0' when omitted after 'flex-shrink', but the original 'auto' was expected. This is a subtle failure where the predicate expects the original implicit value, but the edit changes it.

description: Fails when 'flex' shorthand is expanded with an incorrect 'flex-basis' value.

edits.replace: .flex-container { display: flex; flex-grow: 1; flex-shrink: 0; flex-basis: 0; gap: 10px; }

rationale: The 'flex-basis' property was incorrectly changed from 'auto' to '0', leading to a mismatch with the expected value.

description: Transforms 'grid-template' shorthand to 'grid-template-rows' and 'grid-template-columns' longhands successfully.

edits.search: .grid-container { display: grid; grid-template: 1fr 2fr / auto auto; gap: 8px 16px; }
edits.replace: .grid-container { display: grid; grid-template-rows: 1fr 2fr; grid-template-columns: auto auto; gap: 8px 16px; }

rationale: The 'grid-template' shorthand is correctly expanded into its longhand properties, and the original shorthand is removed.

description: Fails when 'grid-template' shorthand is partially expanded, missing 'grid-template-columns'.

edits.search: .grid-container { display: grid; grid-template: 1fr 2fr / auto auto; gap: 8px 16px; }
edits.replace: .grid-container { display: grid; grid-template-rows: 1fr 2fr; gap: 8px 16px; }

rationale: The 'grid-template-columns' property is missing after the edit, leading to a default value that does not match the original shorthand's implied value.

description: Fails when 'grid-template' shorthand is expanded with an incorrect 'grid-template-columns' value.

edits.search: .grid-container { display: grid; grid-template: 1fr 2fr / auto auto; gap: 8px 16px; }
edits.replace: .grid-container { display: grid; grid-template-rows: 1fr 2fr; grid-template-columns: 100px; gap: 8px 16px; }

rationale: The 'grid-template-columns' property was incorrectly changed from 'auto auto' to '100px', leading to a mismatch with the expected value.

description: Transforms 'list-style' shorthand to 'list-style-type', 'list-style-position', 'list-style-image' longhands successfully.

edits.replace: .team-list { list-style-type: decimal; list-style-position: outside; list-style-image: none; padding-left: 2rem; }

rationale: The 'list-style' shorthand is correctly expanded into its longhand properties, including implicit default values.

description: Transforms 'list-style' shorthand with all values to longhands successfully.

edits.replace: .list-custom { list-style-type: square; list-style-position: inside; list-style-image: url('marker.png'); }

rationale: The 'list-style' shorthand with explicit values is correctly expanded into its longhand properties.

description: Fails when 'list-style' shorthand is partially expanded, missing 'list-style-position'.

edits.replace: .team-list { list-style-type: decimal; list-style-image: none; padding-left: 2rem; }

rationale: The 'list-style-position' property is missing after the edit, leading to a default value that does not match the original shorthand's implied value.

description: Fails when 'list-style' shorthand is expanded with an incorrect 'list-style-type' value.

edits.replace: .list-custom { list-style-type: circle; list-style-position: inside; list-style-image: url('marker.png'); }

rationale: The 'list-style-type' property was incorrectly changed from 'square' to 'circle', leading to a mismatch with the expected value.

description: Transforms 'text-decoration' shorthand to its four longhand properties successfully.

edits.replace: .text-deco { text-decoration-line: underline; text-decoration-style: wavy; text-decoration-color: red; text-decoration-thickness: initial; }

rationale: The 'text-decoration' shorthand is correctly expanded into its four longhand properties, including the implicit 'initial' for thickness.

description: Fails when 'text-decoration' shorthand is partially expanded, missing 'text-decoration-style'.

edits.replace: .text-deco { text-decoration-line: underline; text-decoration-color: red; text-decoration-thickness: initial; }

rationale: The 'text-decoration-style' property is missing after the edit, leading to a default value that does not match the original shorthand's implied value.

description: Fails when 'text-decoration' shorthand is expanded with an incorrect 'text-decoration-style' value.

edits.replace: .text-deco { text-decoration-line: underline; text-decoration-style: solid; text-decoration-color: red; text-decoration-thickness: initial; }

rationale: The 'text-decoration-style' property was incorrectly changed from 'wavy' to 'solid', leading to a mismatch with the expected value.

description: Transforms 'overflow' shorthand to 'overflow-x' and 'overflow-y' longhands successfully.

edits.replace: .overflow-box { overflow-x: hidden; overflow-y: hidden; width: 200px; height: 100px; }

rationale: The 'overflow' shorthand is correctly expanded into its 'overflow-x' and 'overflow-y' longhand properties.

description: Transforms 'overflow' shorthand with two values to 'overflow-x' and 'overflow-y' longhands successfully.

edits.replace: .overflow-scroll { overflow-x: auto; overflow-y: auto; }

rationale: The 'overflow' shorthand with two values is correctly expanded into its 'overflow-x' and 'overflow-y' longhand properties.

description: Fails when 'overflow' shorthand is partially expanded, missing 'overflow-y'.

edits.replace: .overflow-box { overflow-x: hidden; width: 200px; height: 100px; }

rationale: The 'overflow-y' property is missing after the edit, leading to a default value that does not match the original shorthand's implied value.

description: Fails when 'overflow' shorthand is expanded with an incorrect 'overflow-y' value.

edits.replace: .overflow-box { overflow-x: hidden; overflow-y: scroll; width: 200px; height: 100px; }

rationale: The 'overflow-y' property was incorrectly changed from 'hidden' to 'scroll', leading to a mismatch with the expected value.

description: Verify color-mix() resolves to its computed sRGB value correctly.
rationale: The color-mix(in srgb, red 50%, blue) should resolve to an sRGB color that is 50% red and 50% blue, which is rgb(128, 0, 128).

description: Verify color-mix() resolution fails when an incorrect computed value is asserted.
rationale: The color-mix(in srgb, red 50%, blue) should resolve to rgb(128, 0, 128), not pure red.

description: Verify color-mix() resolves correctly after an edit changes its parameters.

edits.replace: color: color-mix(in srgb, green 25%, yellow);

rationale: color-mix(in srgb, green 25%, yellow) should resolve to 25% green (rgb(0,255,0)) and 75% yellow (rgb(255,255,0)), resulting in rgb(64, 255, 0).

description: Verify color-mix() resolution fails when an incorrect computed value is asserted after an edit.

edits.replace: color: color-mix(in srgb, green 25%, yellow);

rationale: After the edit, the color-mix function changes, so the old computed value should no longer be present.

description: Verify CSS nested selector resolution fails when an incorrect style is asserted for the resolved selector.
rationale: The '.child' element explicitly sets its color to blue, overriding the parent's red color.

description: Verify CSS nested selector resolves correctly after an edit changes its style.

edits.replace: .nested-rule { color: red; & .child { color: green; } }

rationale: After the edit, the nested rule for '.child' should apply 'color: green;'.

description: Verify that changing a parent rule does not incorrectly affect a specifically nested child rule.

edits.replace: .nested-rule { color: purple; & .child { color: blue; } }

rationale: Even if the parent's color changes, the child's explicitly set color should remain 'blue' due to higher specificity.

description: Verify @property for <color> syntax correctly applies a valid color value.

edits.replace: .edge-hero { color: #2c3e50; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: bold; } @property --test-color { syntax: "<color>"; initial-value: red; inherits: false; }
.edge-card { background: #ecf0f1; padding: 1rem; margin: 0.5rem 0; border-radius: 4px; --test-color: blue; }

rationale: A custom property registered with <color> syntax should accept and apply a valid color value.

description: Verify @property for <color> syntax rejects an invalid value (e.g., length).

edits.replace: .edge-hero { color: #2c3e50; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: bold; } @property --test-color { syntax: "<color>"; initial-value: red; inherits: false; }
.edge-card { background: #ecf0f1; padding: 1rem; margin: 0.5rem 0; border-radius: 4px; --test-color: 100px; }

rationale: A custom property with <color> syntax should not accept '100px' as a valid value. The property should fall back to its initial value or be invalid.

description: Verify @property for <color> syntax falls back to initial-value on invalid assignment.

edits.replace: .edge-hero { color: #2c3e50; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: bold; } @property --test-color { syntax: "<color>"; initial-value: red; inherits: false; }
.edge-card { background: #ecf0f1; padding: 1rem; margin: 0.5rem 0; border-radius: 4px; --test-color: 100px; }

rationale: When an invalid value ('100px') is assigned to a custom property with <color> syntax, it should fall back to its 'initial-value' ('red').

description: Verify @property for <length> syntax correctly applies a valid length value and is used by another property.

edits.replace: .edge-hero { color: #2c3e50; font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: bold; } @property --test-length { syntax: "<length>"; initial-value: 1rem; inherits: true; }
.edge-card { background: #ecf0f1; padding: 1rem; margin: 0.5rem 0; border-radius: 4px; --test-length: 20px; padding: var(--test-length); }

rationale: A custom property registered with <length> syntax should accept '20px' and be correctly used by the 'padding' property.

description: Verify 'subgrid' on grid-template-columns correctly inherits computed tracks from parent.

edits.replace: Clamped width
.color-mix-test { color: color-mix(in srgb, red 50%, blue); } .parent-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 2fr 100px; } .child-subgrid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: subgrid; }

rationale: A child element with 'grid-template-columns: subgrid' should inherit the computed track definition from its parent grid.

description: Verify 'subgrid' on grid-template-columns resolves to computed tracks, not the literal 'subgrid' keyword.

edits.replace: Clamped width
.color-mix-test { color: color-mix(in srgb, red 50%, blue); } .parent-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 2fr 100px; } .child-subgrid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: subgrid; }

rationale: The 'subgrid' keyword is a transformation instruction; the computed value should be the actual track definition, not the keyword itself.

description: Verify 'subgrid' updates its computed tracks when the parent's grid-template-columns changes.

edits.replace: Clamped width
.color-mix-test { color: color-mix(in srgb, red 50%, blue); } .parent-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; } .child-subgrid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: subgrid; }

rationale: The child's 'subgrid' should dynamically reflect changes in the parent's grid-template-columns.

description: Verify 'subgrid' fails to match old computed tracks after parent's grid-template-columns changes.

edits.replace: Clamped width
.color-mix-test { color: color-mix(in srgb, red 50%, blue); } .parent-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; } .child-subgrid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: subgrid; }

rationale: The child's 'subgrid' should update, so it should not match the previous parent's track definition.

description: Verify clamp() resolves to its computed pixel value based on an assumed viewport width.
rationale: Assuming a 1000px viewport, clamp(200px, 50%, 800px) resolves to 500px (50% of 1000px is 500px, which is between 200px and 800px).

description: Verify clamp() resolves to a new computed pixel value after an edit changes its parameters.

edits.replace: width: clamp(100px, 75%, 600px);

rationale: Assuming a 1000px viewport, clamp(100px, 75%, 600px) resolves to 600px (75% of 1000px is 750px, capped by max of 600px).

description: Verify clamp() resolution fails when asserting the old computed value after an edit.

edits.replace: width: clamp(100px, 75%, 600px);

rationale: After the edit, the clamp function's parameters change, so the old computed value (500px) should no longer be valid.

description: Scoped style applies within its boundary but is incorrectly detected outside the boundary, leading to a false positive detection.

edits.replace:
table.data-table td { padding: 0.5rem; border-bottom: 1px solid #eee; }
    @scope (.hero) { p { color: green; } }

rationale: The predicate expects the 'p' inside '.card' to also be green, which should not happen if @scope is working correctly. This scenario tests if the system incorrectly applies the scoped style outside its boundary or if the predicate correctly identifies the style not being applied.

description: Scoped style correctly applies within its boundary and does not leak outside, verifying expected @scope behavior.

edits.replace:
table.data-table td { padding: 0.5rem; border-bottom: 1px solid #eee; }
    @scope (.hero) { p { color: green; } }

rationale: This scenario confirms that the 'p' element within '.hero' correctly receives the scoped style, while 'p' elements outside this scope (e.g., within '.card') retain their original styles, demonstrating correct @scope boundary enforcement.

description: Nested @scope rule applies to its specific target within the outer scope, and the outer scope's styles do not leak to unrelated elements. A predicate expects a leak, leading to failure.

edits.replace:
table.data-table td { padding: 0.5rem; border-bottom: 1px solid #eee; }
    @scope (.hero) { p { color: green; } @scope (.hero-title) { span { font-style: italic; } } }

rationale: The scenario introduces nested @scope rules. The first two predicates verify correct application within the respective scopes. The third predicate expects a style from the inner scope to leak to an element outside both scopes, which should fail, testing strict boundary adherence.

description: @scope rule with a 'to' clause correctly limits style application to elements before the specified upper boundary.

edits.replace:
table.data-table td { padding: 0.5rem; border-bottom: 1px solid #eee; }
    @scope (.hero) to (.card) { p { border: 1px solid red; } }

rationale: This scenario tests the 'to' clause of @scope, ensuring that the style for 'p' elements is applied only within '.hero' and stops before the first '.card' element, as intended. The predicates confirm both the application within the scope and the non-application outside the upper boundary.

description: SQL injection via custom DB method name and variable assignment, bypassing specific regex patterns.

predicates.message: Should detect SQL injection via custom method and variable

rationale: The gate's SQL injection scanner looks for `query|execute|run` methods and direct concatenation of `req.|params.|body.|query.` into SQL. This scenario uses a `db.fetch` method, which is not in the regex, and assigns `req.query.id` to a variable first, bypassing the direct user input source check.

description: SQL injection via user input from `req.headers`, a source not covered by the gate's regex.

predicates.message: Should detect SQL injection from req.headers

rationale: The gate's SQL injection scanner specifically looks for user input from `req.`, `params.`, `body.`, or `query.`. It does not include `req.headers`, `req.cookies`, or other less common input sources, leading to a false negative.

description: SQL injection using `array.join('')` for string concatenation, bypassing explicit `+` and template literal checks.

predicates.message: Should detect SQL injection via array.join

rationale: The gate's SQL injection scanner primarily targets string concatenation using the `+` operator or template literals (`${...}`). Using `array.join('')` to construct the SQL query bypasses these specific regex patterns, resulting in a false negative.

description: Open redirect vulnerability using `res.writeHead` and `Location` header with user-supplied URL.

predicates.message: Should detect open redirect via Location header

rationale: The gate does not include a specific scanner for 'open_redirect' vulnerabilities. This scenario introduces a classic server-side open redirect using `res.writeHead` and the `Location` header, which the gate will consequently miss.

description: Client-side open redirect via `window.location` assignment with user input, bypassing XSS checks.
rationale: The gate lacks a specific scanner for 'open_redirect'. Additionally, its XSS scanner focuses on `innerHTML`, `document.write`, `eval`, and `dangerouslySetInnerHTML`, but does not explicitly check for `window.location` assignments, allowing this client-side redirect to be missed.

description: Open redirect where user input forms a path component, leading to a bypass if not properly validated.

predicates.message: Should detect open redirect via path component

rationale: The gate lacks a specific scanner for 'open_redirect'. This scenario demonstrates a common pattern where a user-controlled path segment is concatenated into a redirect URL, which can be exploited for open redirect if the path is crafted (e.g., `//evil.com`). The gate will not detect this.

description: Secret read from `.env` file and exposed in an API response, bypassing the hardcoded secret scanner.

edits.replace: res.end(JSON.stringify({ echo: body, timestamp: Date.now(), secret_key: process.env.SECRET_KEY })); // Leaking secret from .env

predicates.message: Should detect secret leaked in response

rationale: The `scanSecrets` function explicitly skips `.env` files, assuming they are for configuration and not direct code leaks. It also performs static analysis, so it cannot detect secrets being read from environment variables at runtime and then exposed in an API response body.

description: Hardcoded secret split across multiple variables and concatenated, bypassing single-literal regex detection.

predicates.message: Should detect split and concatenated secret

rationale: The gate's `scanSecrets` regexes look for long string literals matching secret patterns. By splitting a secret into multiple shorter string literals and then concatenating them, the individual parts may not meet the length criteria, and the concatenated result is not a literal string for the regex to match, leading to a false negative.

description: False positive: A long, non-secret public identifier string is flagged as a hardcoded secret.

edits.replace:
const PORT = process.env.PORT || 3000;

const publicToken = 'this-is-a-public-token-for-a-resource-not-a-secret-api-key-or-password-it-is-publicly-visible'; // False positive candidate

predicates.message: Should not flag public token as secret

rationale: The gate's `scanSecrets` regex `(?:secret|token)\s*[:=]\s*['"][A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,}['"]/gi` is overly broad. It matches the variable name `publicToken` followed by `=` and a long string literal, even though the string is explicitly described as a public identifier and not a secret, leading to a false positive.

description: Missing rate limit on the existing `/api/echo` POST endpoint, which processes user input.

edits.replace:
if (req.url === '/api/echo' && req.method === 'POST') {
    // This endpoint processes user input and could be abused without rate limiting.

predicates.message: Should detect missing rate limit on /api/echo

rationale: The gate does not have a scanner for `rate_limiting` vulnerabilities. The `/api/echo` endpoint is a POST request handler that processes the request body, making it susceptible to abuse like spamming or resource exhaustion if not rate-limited.

description: Missing rate limit on a newly introduced sensitive 'reset password' endpoint.

predicates.message: Should detect missing rate limit on password reset

rationale: The gate does not have a scanner for `rate_limiting` vulnerabilities. A password reset endpoint is a critical security function that requires robust rate limiting to prevent brute-force attacks, account enumeration, and spamming, which the gate will miss.

description: Missing rate limit on the existing `/api/items` GET endpoint, potentially leading to Denial of Service.

edits.replace:
if (req.url === '/api/items') {
    // This endpoint fetches data and could be targeted for Denial of Service if not rate-limited.

predicates.message: Should detect missing rate limit on /api/items

rationale: The gate does not have a scanner for `rate_limiting` vulnerabilities. Even a GET endpoint, such as `/api/items`, can be a target for Denial of Service (DoS) attacks if it's not rate-limited, especially if it performs resource-intensive operations on the backend.
